proven.claim: PROVE: when transitionCount > 0, isVacuous returns false (result=0).
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; LINE: 72
; PROVE: when transitionCount > 0, isVacuous returns false (result=0).
; The return value is exactly (transitionCount === 0).
(declare-const num_declares Int)
(declare-const num_asserts Int)
(declare-const transition_count Int)
(declare-const result Int)
; Guard: past early-return (non-empty declares and asserts)
(assert (> num_declares 0))
(assert (> num_asserts 0))
; Code: transitionCount initialized to 0, only incremented → non-negative
(assert (>= transition_count 0))
; Code: return (transitionCount === 0)
(assert (=> (> transition_count 0) (= result 0)))
(assert (=> (= transition_count 0) (= result 1)))
; Negate contract: result=1 (vacuous) despite transitionCount > 0
(assert (> transition_count 0))
(assert (= result 1))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: VIOLATION: false negative — isVacuous returns false for a genuinely vacuous block.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 72
; VIOLATION: false negative — isVacuous returns false for a genuinely vacuous block.
; Cause: (assert (and (> x 0) (< y 0))) co-references x and y in one string →
; inner loop finds both names → referencedVars=2 → transitionCount++ →
; return false. But there is no arithmetic dependency between x and y.
(declare-const x_appears Int)          ; 1 if "x" found via \bx\b in the assertion string
(declare-const y_appears Int)          ; 1 if "y" found via \by\b
(declare-const referenced_vars Int)    ; x_appears + y_appears, computed by inner loop
(declare-const transition_count Int)   ; incremented when referenced_vars >= 2
(declare-const result Int)             ; 0 = false (non-vacuous), 1 = true (vacuous)
; Code: both variable names appear in the assertion string
(assert (= x_appears 1))
(assert (= y_appears 1))
; Code: inner loop computes referenced_vars by counting matches
(assert (= referenced_vars (+ x_appears y_appears)))
; Code: referenced_vars >= 2 → transitionCount is incremented from 0 to 1
(assert (>= referenced_vars 2))
(assert (= transition_count 1))
; Code: return (transitionCount === 0) → false, so result=0
(assert (=> (> transition_count 0) (= result 0)))
(assert (= result 0))
; The block is vacuous: (and (> x 0) (< y 0)) has no arithmetic relationship x↔y
; yet the function classifies it as non-vacuous
(check-sat)

violations.claim: VIOLATION: false positive — isVacuous returns true for a block that IS a genuine transition.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 72
; VIOLATION: false positive — isVacuous returns true for a block that IS a genuine transition.
; Cause: (assert (= x (- x 1))) references only one declared variable name "x" →
; referencedVars=1 < 2 → transitionCount stays 0 → return true (vacuous).
; But (= x (- x 1)) IS a state mutation: it constrains x relative to itself.
(declare-const x_appears Int)          ; 1 — "x" found in "(assert (= x (- x 1)))"
(declare-const referenced_vars Int)    ; = x_appears = 1 (only one declared variable)
(declare-const transition_count Int)   ; stays 0 — threshold not reached
(declare-const result Int)             ; 1 = true (vacuous)
; Code: only "x" matches in the assertion string
(assert (= x_appears 1))
; Code: inner loop finds exactly one declared name
(assert (= referenced_vars x_appears))
; Code: referenced_vars = 1 < 2 → the conditional is not entered
(assert (< referenced_vars 2))
(assert (= transition_count 0))
; Code: return (transitionCount === 0) → true
(assert (= result 1))
; Violation: function says "vacuous" for a block containing (assert (= x (- x 1)))
; which is a genuine self-referential transition
(check-sat)
